dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal uses longer-term MA (sma_50/sma_200 for trend identification) combined with shorter-period RSI (rsi_14) for entry timing. Direction is context-dependent based on trend direction. Exit conditions inferred as RSI reaching overbought/oversold extremes since author provided no explicit exit rules. Used rsi_14 as the shorter-period RSI option available; stochastic not used as RSI provides clearer signal. Trend confirmation via moving average crossover serves as trend line proxy.

backtest_rule.reason: Signal uses longer-term MA (sma_50/sma_200 for trend identification) combined with shorter-period RSI (rsi_14) for entry timing. Direction is context-dependent based on trend direction. Exit conditions inferred as RSI reaching overbought/oversold extremes since author provided no explicit exit rules. Used rsi_14 as the shorter-period RSI option available; stochastic not used as RSI provides clearer signal. Trend confirmation via moving average crossover serves as trend line proxy.

source_rule_text: Combine a longer-term moving average to identify trend with a shorter-period RSI or stochastic for improved entry timing, tuning both indicators to work together.
